title: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream

seo.title: Rajas con Crema Recipe: Authentic Mexican Roasted Pepper Cream
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Rajas con Crema, a creamy Mexican roasted poblano pepper dish. This easy vegetarian recipe is perfect as a side or main. Get the best Rajas con Crema recipe!
longTailKeywords: how to make Rajas con Crema
Rajas con Crema with poblano peppers
best Rajas con Crema at home
seo.h1: Authentic Rajas con Crema Recipe: Creamy Roasted Poblano Peppers
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Rajas con Crema
How to Make Rajas con Crema: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Best Rajas con Crema
Serving Suggestions for Rajas con Crema
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy Rajas con Crema, a traditional Mexican vegetarian dish made with roasted poblano peppers and cream.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, smoky flavors of this homemade Rajas con Crema, a beloved Mexican comfort food.

description: Rajas con Crema is a beloved Mexican comfort food featuring tender strips of roasted poblano peppers bathed in a rich, creamy sauce. This vegetarian delight offers a perfect balance of smoky, mildly spicy, and savory flavors, making it an incredibly satisfying dish. It's a versatile side that elevates any meal, bringing a taste of authentic Mexico to your table.

ingredients.notes: roasted, peeled, seeded, and sliced into strips (rajas)
or heavy cream/sour cream thinned with milk
if using sour cream or for thinning

instructions.title: Roast the Poblanos
instructions.text: Preheat your broiler or a comal/griddle to high. Place the poblano peppers directly on the hot surface or under the broiler, turning frequently until the skin is charred and blistered all over. This usually takes 10-15 minutes.

instructions.title: Sweat and Peel Peppers
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the charred poblanos to a bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap or place them in a sealed paper bag for 10 minutes. This steaming process helps loosen the skin. Once cooled, peel off the skin, remove the stems, seeds, and veins, then slice the peppers into thin strips (rajas).

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large skillet over medium heat, add vegetable oil. Once hot, add the sliced white onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Combine and Cook
instructions.text: Add the poblano strips (rajas) and corn to the skillet with the onions and garlic. Sauté for 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Create the Cream Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the Crema Mexicana (or thinned sour cream/heavy cream) and milk, if using. Stir well to combine all ingredients. Add the chicken or vegetable bouillon powder and salt to taste. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Simmer and Serve
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low and let the Rajas con Crema simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly and the flavors to deepen. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Serve hot.

tips: For the best flavor, make sure to properly char and sweat your poblano peppers. This step is crucial for easy peeling and that authentic smoky taste.
If you cant find Crema Mexicana, a good substitute is a mix of sour cream and a little milk to thin it to a similar consistency.
Rajas con Crema is fantastic served with warm tortillas, as a side for grilled meats, or even as a filling for tacos and quesadillas.
article: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream is a beloved Mexican Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
